claim: Problem awareness, the foundation of delegation, requires clearly defining goals and understanding what work is needed before involving AI
actionable: Before delegating to AI, spend time documenting project goals, success criteria, and required thinking patterns to guide AI effectively
source_title: Lesson 4: A closer look at Delegation | AI Fluency: Framework & Foundations Course

claim: Platform awareness requires hands-on experimentation with different AI systems to understand which models prioritize speed over depth or accuracy over creativity
actionable: Regularly experiment with multiple AI systems on your specific use cases to build intuition about their relative strengths and limitations
source_title: Lesson 4: A closer look at Delegation | AI Fluency: Framework & Foundations Course

claim: Task delegation involves deciding which specific parts of workflow would benefit from automation, which need augmentation, and which require exclusively human judgment
actionable: Map each workflow step to one of three categories: full automation, human-AI augmentation, or exclusive human work with explicit reasoning
source_title: Lesson 4: A closer look at Delegation | AI Fluency: Framework & Foundations Course

claim: Effective AI collaboration isn't about handing over control but about making thoughtful choices that leverage unique strengths of both human and artificial intelligence
actionable: Maintain human agency in AI collaborations by treating every delegation as a strategic choice rather than default automation
source_title: Lesson 4: A closer look at Delegation | AI Fluency: Framework & Foundations Course

claim: The most effective AI collaborators are experts in their fields first and AI delegators second; domain expertise is the cornerstone of good delegation
actionable: Build deep domain expertise as a prerequisite for effective AI collaboration; expert judgment guides what tasks are worth automating
source_title: Lesson 4: A closer look at Delegation | AI Fluency: Framework & Foundations Course
